data.title: Xcel Energy plans to add 5,168 MW of new generation capacity in Texas and New Mexico by 2030, including 521 MW extensions.
data.description: Xcel Energy recently revealed its plans to address the increasing energy demands in Texas and New Mexico by adding new generation resources to its portfolio. The expansion includes extensions of 521 megawatts in existing generation and the introduction of 17 new power projects, which will collectively contribute 5,168 megawatts of new nameplate capacity to the area's generation fleet by 2030. The selection of technologies encompasses 3,200 megawatts of dispatchable generation to cater to the growing energy needs in the region.

Chinese Embassy in Ottawa: If Canada cancels discriminatory tariff measures, China's countermeasures can also be adjusted.
Chinese Embassy in Ottawa States that The Outcome of the Antidumping Probe on Canadian Canola Was Due by September but Might Be Extended by Half a Year

data.title: Rail teamsters and allied unions host rail safety town hall near East Palestine to highlight rail safety on July 19 at 12 p.m. in Columbiana, Ohio.
data.description: In Columbiana, Ohio, on July 18, 2025, Rail Workers, Union Leaders, and Elected Officials gathered to emphasize the importance of Rail Safety. The Teamsters Rail Conference, representing the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen (BLET) and the Brotherhood of... The event took place at 12 p.m. on Saturday, July 19.

data.title: California's Q1 2025 local sales tax receipts up 0.34% from 2024, after 8 quarters of decline. Economic uncertainty persists.
data.description: In Brea, California, a recent analysis by HdL Companies revealed that California's local one-cent sales and use tax revenue for the first quarter of 2025 saw a slight increase of 0.34% when compared to the same period in 2024, after accounting for anomalies. This growth comes after eight consecutive quarters of decline. Despite this positive trend, HdL economists warn that the uptick may be short-lived due to ongoing economic instability. Local governments are facing unprecedented challenges amid the uncertain economic landscape.

data.title: MEI Pharma (NASDAQ: MEIP) stock surges with 35.64M volume vs. 29.8K avg. Raised $100M through 29.2M shares at $3.42 each.
data.description: MEI Pharma, Inc. (NASDAQ: MEIP) saw a surge in stock trading on Friday, with a session volume of 35.64 million shares, significantly higher than the average volume of 29.8K shares. The company recently engaged in a private investment in public equity (PIPE) offering, involving the purchase and sale of 29.2 million shares at $3.42 per share. This move is expected to generate around $100 million in aggregate gross proceeds. As part of the transaction's closure, MEI will bring Charlie Lee on board as... *For the complete article, please refer to the original source.

data.title: Nasdaq responds to proxy filed by Invesco QQQ Trust with proposals: statement issued on July 18, 2025.
data.description: On July 18, 2025, Nasdaq released a statement regarding the preliminary proxy submitted by Invesco QQQ Trust. The document outlined various proposals put forth by the Trust.

data.title: 21Shares US LLC files for 21Shares FTSE Crypto 10 and 21Shares FTSE Crypto 10 ex-BTC Index ETFs with SEC in New York.
data.description: On July 18, 2025, 21Shares US LLC revealed the submission of a registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for the 21Shares FTSE Crypto 10 Index ETF and the 21Shares FTSE Crypto 10 ex-BTC Index ETF. This announcement marks a significant development in the finance sector, with the company taking steps to establish investment opportunities through these two Funds. The ETFs are poised to offer exposure to the growing digital asset market, catering to investors looking to diversify their portfolios. This move aligns with the increasing interest in cryptocurrencies, indicating a strategic move by 21Shares US LLC to tap into this lucrative market segment.

data.title: Stallion Uranium settles debt with Atha Energy Corp, issues 802,809 shares at $0.135 per share.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on July 18, 2025, Stallion Uranium Corp. settled its debt with Atha Energy Corp. by issuing 802,809 common shares at $0.135 per share. Stallion's stock symbols are TSX-V: STUD, OTCQB: STLNF, and FSE: FE0. This settlement follows a news release from May 22, 2025, and took place on July 16, 2025.

data.title: Real estate sector faces extreme weakness, underperforming S&P 500, with VNQ ETF down 5% in 2025. Real estate has lost 75% value compared to 2007 highs relative to S&P 500. P/E ratio for S&P 500 real estate sector is 35.04, making it second highest after tech.
data.description: The real estate sector is currently experiencing significant weakness after years of underperformance, with prices at levels not seen since the global financial crisis. The Vanguard Real Estate ETF (VNQ) has underperformed the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) by 5% in 2025, marking the ninth time in the past decade the sector has fallen behind the broader market. Real estate has lost about 75% of its value compared to its 2007 highs against the S&P 500, surpassing the decline seen during the 2008 housing crisis. Despite this, investors are not showing much interest in the sector. Real estate stocks are not considered cheap at the moment, with the trailing price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio for the S&P 500 real estate sector at 35.04 as of July 16, ranking only behind the tech sector at 35.48. The forward P/E ratio also indicates that real estate stocks are not trading at bargain prices.

data.title: Stocks slip after Netflix disappoint, Bitcoin looks down; Wall Street cautious amid trade tensions. Dow drops 250 points.
data.description: The week on Wall Street concluded cautiously after Netflix Inc. (NASDAQ: NFLX) reported earnings that disappointed investors. Ongoing trade tensions kept investors on the sidelines, preventing significant gains in tech-heavy indexes near record levels. The S&P 500 hovered around the 6,300 mark, unable to break through for the past two weeks. The Nasdaq 100 dipped to 23,040, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped by 250 points, representing a 0.5% decline by midday trading in New York. President Donald Trump is advocating for a substantial 15% to 20% minimum tariff on all European Union goods, and opposing any reduction in the current 25% tariffs on EU car imports. If no deal is reached by Aug. 1, he threatened a 30% blanket tariff on EU imports. Despite the somber atmosphere on Wall Street, recent data from Main Street showed promise, with the University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index reaching a five-month high in June.
India Maintains Anti-Dumping Duty on China’s Aniline to Protect Domestic Market, Says Bloomberg

data.title: In 2019, trader invested $10,000 in XRP at $0.1835, now worth $186,000, marking 185x return. Prominent success story.
data.description: XRP, a controversial altcoin in the cryptocurrency world, has shown significant gains for a trader. In December 2019, CrediBULL Crypto boldly predicted that the XRP investment of $10,000 at $0.1835 could buy him a Lamborghini within 4 years. Despite anticipating price drops to $0.13-$0.15, he held onto the investment as a long-term strategy. Nearly five years later, the investment has soared to $186,000, providing a remarkable 185x return on the initial amount. This success story exemplifies the unpredictable yet rewarding nature of the crypto market.
New York Federal Reserve Accepts Full $199.298 Billion of Submitted Funds for Reverse Repo on July 18

data.title: Alibaba stock rebounds, exceeds 50-day moving average after Nvidia news boosts gains in China.
data.description: Shares of Alibaba surged, surpassing its 50-day moving average, in response to reports that Nvidia has secured permission to distribute some of its AI chips in China. This development led to a significant increase in Alibaba's stock value, marking a resurgence above a key threshold.

data.title: Microsoft's stock hits record high before earnings report; Analysts optimistic; Market watching closely.
data.description: Financial analysts on Wall Street are showing growing optimism about the performance of Microsoft's stock leading up to the company's upcoming earnings report for the June quarter. Microsoft's stock is currently reaching record highs before the earnings announcement, indicating positive expectations from investors.

data.title: Stock market reaches new highs driven by Nvidia, Taiwan Semi earnings, AI chipmakers, and solid economic data; online brokers are also surging.
data.description: The stock market surged to new record levels this week, driven by the strong performance of Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, and AI chipmakers, as well as positive economic data. Online brokerage firms also saw a rise in activity. Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor particularly stood out with robust earnings reports, contributing to the overall bullish sentiment in the market. This week's performance indicated a positive trend for the stock market, with key players in the technology and semiconductor sectors leading the way. The upturn in the market was fueled by a combination of factors, including the impressive performance of specific companies, solid economic indicators, and increased interest in online trading platforms.

data.title: Talen Energy's stock soars by over 20% due to $3.5 billion deal to enhance data center load. Constellation Energy and Vistra see gains as well.
data.description: Shares of Constellation Energy and Vistra, both part of the S&P 500 index, approached favorable purchase levels as Talen Energy surged by over 20%. Talen Energy's significant increase was driven by a $3.5 billion deal aimed at improving data center capacity. This led to a notable rise in the stock prices of the mentioned companies, according to the latest reports.
